items.title: Saoedische club blokkeert Anderlecht-avontuur Schreuder voorlopig
items.summary: Alfred Schreuder (53) was dicht bij een avontuur bij Anderlecht, maar vooralsnog zit de overstap er nog niet in. De huidige werkgever van de trainer, Al-Diraiyah FC, blokkeert de transfer namelijk. Dat bevestigen ingewijden.

items.title: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Arsenal: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky | And email Scott 2 min: It’s not a pleasant evening weather-wise. It’s raining in the West Midlands, and that rain could turn to sleet or snow later. Slapstick entertainment not yet off the menu. Wolves get the ball rolling. “A quiet night is wanted, I think, from all parties, at least in the sense of avoiding the ghastly shenanigans on view in the notorious match last night,” begins Charles Antaki, who speaks for us all. “Raucous is good, animated is fine, full bloodedness welcome, but none of the other stuff, please. British football has been pretty free of it these last years, for which we should all be grateful.” Continue reading...

items.title: Arsenal v Leuven: Women’s Champions League – live
items.summary: ⚽ WCL updates from the second leg (first leg: 0-4) ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky | Email Sarah There is going to be a revival of el Clasico in the quarter-finals as Real Madrid secured their spot in the last eight where they will play Barcelona. Real beat Paris FC 5-2 on aggregate to go through. I would love to hear from you. Anything from how you think this tie will shake out, what you might be giving up for Lent or, on the opposite end of the scale, what you are treating yourself to in terms of snacks while watching the game. Email me and let me know. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Saka raakt weer geblesseerd, assist Poku in Griekenland
items.summary: Woensdagavond staan er weer vier wedstrijden in de tussenronde van de Champions League op het programma. Onder meer Newcastle United, Internazionale en Atlético Madrid komen in actie. Daarnaast spelen Arsenal en AC Milan competitieduels. Volg het voetbal in de VI Live.

items.title: Gordon ruziet, maar breekt clubrecord Shearer op historische avond Newcastle
items.summary: Anthony Gordon (24) kende een wervelende avond in Azerbeidzjan. Op bezoek bij Qarabag FK scoorde de Brit vier keer, waardoor Newcastle United uithaalde: 1-6. Ondertussen schoot de aanvaller ook clubicoon Alan Shearer nog uit de boeken.

items.title: Villarreal neemt dankzij Mikautadze afstand van Atlético Madrid
items.summary: Villarreal heeft woensdagavond een overwinning geboekt op Levante. Daarvoor was Georges Mikautadze verantwoordelijk. Door de zege heeft Villarreal de derde plek in La Liga nu steviger in handen.

items.title: 'UEFA steunt plannen voor uitbreiding van WK voor clubs'
items.summary: De plannen van de FIFA om het WK voor clubs uit te breiden naar 48 deelnemers krijgen groen licht vanuit de UEFA. De Europese voetbalbond, die eerder nog bezwaren had tegen een groter toernooi, is bereid het voorstel voor de editie van 2029 te steunen. Voorwaarde is wel dat het WK voor clubs geen tweejaarlijks evenement wordt.

items.title: Vinícius Jr. racism allegation prompts UEFA probe
items.summary: UEFA will investigate the racism allegations made by Vinícius Júnior in Real Madrid's game against Benfica.

items.title: Gordon makes UCL history with 4 goals in 1st half
items.summary: Newcastle United forward Anthony Gordon has become just the second player to score four goals in the first half of a UEFA Champions League game and the first to do so in the knockout stage.

items.title: Real Madrid book UWCL quarterfinal with Barça
items.summary: Real Madrid set up a Clásico against three-time champion Barcelona in the women's Champions League quarterfinals by beating Paris FC 2-0 on Wednesday.

items.title: Van Dijk backs Szoboszlai as future L'pool leader
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk believes midfielder Dominik Szoboszlai has all the qualities to one day succeed him as Liverpool captain having led by example this season.

items.title: 'Flourishing in his new central role!' | Saka opener ends 15-game goalless run
items.summary: Bukayo Saka opens the scoring for Arsenal against Wolves to end his 15 game goalless run.

items.title: Anthony Gordon grabs four as Newcastle hit Qarabag for six in playoff
items.summary: When, before kick-off, Qarabag’s manager Gurban Gurbanov declared that Newcastle possess “a style of play that does not suit us,” there were suggestions he was playing mind games. Long before half-time it was fully apparent that Gurbanov had rather understated things. Had this been a boxing match it would surely have been stopped by the 20th minute. Qarabag were utterly overwhelmed by the pace of their guests and that of Anthony Gordon in particular. Gordon scored four times, boosting his tally in the Champions League this season to 10 goals. Continue reading...

items.title: European soccer live updates: Inter, Atleti in action in UCL; Arsenal lead Wolves
items.summary: Enjoy all the updates from today's knockout phase playoff first legs, with Inter and Atléti in action. Also, Arsenal take on Wolves in the Premier League.

items.title: Mourinho accused of gaslighting for response to Vinícius’ allegation of racism
items.summary: Benfica manager criticised strongly by Kick It Out Uefa investigating Real Madrid player’s claims José Mourinho has been accused of gaslighting for his response to Vinícius Júnior’s allegations of racist abuse. The Benfica manager was criticised strongly by the anti-discrimination charity Kick It Out for referencing the Real Madrid forward’s goal celebration when talking about the incident. Vinícius’s allegation that he was racially abused on Tuesday by Benfica’s Gianluca Prestianni during Madrid’s Champions League playoff win in Lisbon prompted Uefa to appoint an ethics and disciplinary inspector to investigate. Benfica said they would collaborate with the investigation but reiterated their support for Prestianni, who they said was “victim” of a “smear campaign”. Continue reading...

items.title: Leicester appoint Rowett until end of season
items.summary: Championship strugglers Leicester City confirm former player Gary Rowett as their new manager on a deal until the end of the season.

items.title: Vinícius, Mourinho and treating racism as reputational risk rather than a lived reality | Jonathan Liew
items.summary: The Brazilian has seen this before, football has seen this before and yet why does it feel like nothing ever changes? José Mourinho: against provoking opposition fans. José Mourinho: in favour of restrained celebrations. José Mourinho, once of the poke-in-the-eye, sprint-down-the-touchline, accost-the-referee-in-the-car-park school of footballing expression: now apparently very big on showing respect to the game. Well, it seems like we’ve all been on a journey here. “I told him the biggest person in the history of this club was Black,” Mourinho recounted when asked about his conversation with Vinícius Júnior on Tuesday night. “This club, the last thing that it is, is racist.” And doubtless these words will have been a profound source of comfort to Vinícius in his lowest moment, having been insulted on the pitch by an opposition player in a Champions League playoff. Continue reading...

items.title: Vinicius: Eight years at Real Madrid, 20 cases of alleged racist abuse
items.summary: Spanish football expert Guillem Balague details how Vincius Jr has become a global symbol of resistance against discrimination.

items.title: What is Uefa's racism protocol and is it working?
items.summary: Rather than player welfare, the focus was about racism protocol and process. Did this fail Real Madrid's Vinicius Jr against Benfica?

items.title: Rock carrying chalk after blaming Belgian soap for Premier League struggles
items.summary: Josh Rock is ready to wash his hands of Belgian soap after blaming last week's Premier League hammering in Antwerp on a pre-match visit to the toilet.
